records.ety: [L. intercludere, interclusum; inter between + claudere to shut. See Close, and cf. Interclose.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Intercluded; p. pr. & vb. n. Intercluding.]

defs: To shut off or out from a place or course, by something intervening; to intercept; to cut off; to interrupt.
quotes: So all passage of external air into the receiver may be intercluded.
